description_long: This is a classical marble bust sculpture depicting a youthful human figure adorned with a wreath of grape leaves and clusters, suggestive of Bacchus or Dionysus from Greco-Roman mythology. The head is slightly turned to the left, with detailed facial features including a prominent nose, full lips, and deep-set eyes. The hair is intricately carved, with curls and grape clusters encircling the head, adding texture and depth. The bust is mounted on a square, veined stone pedestal with a beveled edge, and the base appears to be made of a different, darker stone. The surface of the sculpture is smooth with subtle variations in color, typical of aged marble. There is an inscription or marking on the front of the pedestal, but no clear artist or date is visible.
description: A classical marble bust of a youthful figure with grapevine wreath, mounted on a veined stone pedestal. The sculpture features detailed facial features and textured hair with grape clusters.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the face, grapevine wreath, and inscription on the pedestal.
description_view_1: Left rear three-quarter view highlighting the back of the head, grape clusters, and the curvature of the bust.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the head, hair details, and the full shape of the pedestal.
description_view_3: Right profile view emphasizing the facial profile, grapevine wreath, and the draped shoulder.
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the background is isolated and neutral.
